971	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
972	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
973	
974	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
975	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
976	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
977	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
978	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
979	
980	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
981	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
982	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
983	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
984	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
985	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
986	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
987	
988	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
989	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
990	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
991	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
992	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
993	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
994	project-local supplement. -/
995	
996	namespace InternalHom
997	
998	open CategoryTheory Limits
999	
1000	universe vC uC
1001	
1002	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1003	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1004	
1005	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1006	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1007	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1008	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1009	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1010	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1011	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1012	
1013	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1014	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1015	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1016	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1017	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1018	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1019	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1020	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1021	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1022	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1023	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1024	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1025	
1026	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1027	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1028	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1029	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1030	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1031	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1032	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1033	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1034	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1035	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1036	    (by
1037	      intro X Y g; ext m
1038	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1039	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1040	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
1041	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1042	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1043	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
1044	
1045	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1046	
1047	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1048	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1049	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1050	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1051	
1052	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1053	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1054	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1055	
1056	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1057	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1058	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1059	
1060	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1061	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1062	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1063	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1064	
1065	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1066	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1067	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1068	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1069	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1070	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1071	
1072	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1073	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1074	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1075	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1076	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1077	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1078	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1079	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1080	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1081	@[implicit_reducible]
1082	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1083	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1084	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1085	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1086	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1087	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1088	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1089	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1090	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1091	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1092	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1093	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1094	  add_smul f g φ := by
1095	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1096	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
1097	
1098	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1099	
1100	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1101	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1102	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1103	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1104	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1105	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1106	applied to that terminal. -/
1107	
1108	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1109	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1110	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1111	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1112	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1113	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1114	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1115	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1116	
1117	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1118	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1119	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1120	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1121	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1122	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) is the remaining downstream build. -/
1123	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1124	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1125	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1126	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
1127	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
1128	
1129	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1130	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
1131	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
1132	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1133	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
1134	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
1135	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1136	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1137	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1138	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
1139	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1140	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
1141	
1142	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
1143	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1144	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1145	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1146	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1147	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1148	  ext1 X; rfl
1149	
1150	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
1151	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1152	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
1153	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
1154	  ext1 X; rfl
1155	
1156	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
1157	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
1158	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
1159	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
1160	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
1161	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
1162	  subst h; rfl
1163	
1164	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
1165	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
1166	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1167	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1168	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
1169	  ext1 X
1170	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
1171	
1172	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
1173	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
1174	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
1175	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1176	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1177	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
1178	  ext1 X
1179	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
1180	
1181	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
1182	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
1183	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1184	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1185	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
1186	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
1187	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
1188	
1189	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
1190	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
1191	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
1192	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
1193	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
1194	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1195	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1196	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1197	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1198	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
1199	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
1200	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1201	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1202	  ext1 Y
1203	  ext m
1204	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1205	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1206	  congr 1
1207	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1208	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1209	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1210	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1211	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1212	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1213	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1214	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1215	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
1216	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1217	    rw [e1, e2]
1218	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1219	  rw [hmor]
1220	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1221	  rfl
1222	
1223	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
1224	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
1225	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
1226	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1227	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1228	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
1229	  toFun := restrictionMap g
1230	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1231	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
1232	
1233	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
1234	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
1235	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1236	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1237	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1238	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1239	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1240	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1241	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1242	  map_id X := by
1243	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1244	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1245	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1246	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1247	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1248	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1249	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
1250	
1251	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
1252	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1253	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1254	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
1255	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
1256	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
1257	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
1258	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
1259	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1260	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
1261	    restrictionMap f.unop
1262	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1263	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1264	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1265	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
1266	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
1267	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1268	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
1269	
1270	end InternalHom
1271	
1272	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1273	
1274	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1275	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1276	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1277	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1278	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1279	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1280	
1281	namespace InternalHom
1282	
1283	section Assembly
1284	
1285	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1286	
1287	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1288	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1289	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1290	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1291	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1292	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1293	noncomputable def internalHom
1294	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1295	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1296	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1297	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1298	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1299	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1300	
1301	end Assembly
1302	
1303	end InternalHom
1304	
1305	end PresheafOfModules
1306	
1307	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
1308	
1309	namespace Scheme
1310	
1311	namespace Modules
1312	
1313	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1314	
1315	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1316	
1317	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1318	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1319	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1320	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1321	
1322	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1323	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1324	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1325	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1326	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1327	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1328	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1329	
1330	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1331	
1332	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1333	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1334	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1335	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1336	
1337	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1338	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1339	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1340	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1341	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1342	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1343	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1344	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1345	
1346	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1347	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1348	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1349	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1350	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1351	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1352	law is definitional. -/
1353	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1354	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1355	
1356	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1357	
1358	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1359	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1360	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1361	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1362	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1363	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1364	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1365	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1366	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1367	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1368	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1369	
1370	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1371	
1372	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1373	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1374	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1375	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1376	
1377	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1378	
1379	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1380	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1381	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1382	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1383	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1384	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1385	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1386	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1387	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1388	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1389	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1390	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1391	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1392	
1393	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1394	sheaf.**
1395	
1396	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1397	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1398	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1399	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1400	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1401	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1402	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1403	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=

1018	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
1019	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
1020	
1021	\emph{Superseded route --- this entire section is off path and is not to be
1022	formalized.} The construction below realizes a \emph{full} symmetric monoidal
1023	category on all of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) by instantiating Mathlib's
1024	localization-of-monoidal-categories API. It was the original plan for supplying the
1025	associator. The relative Picard group law, however, is a structure on
1026	\emph{isomorphism classes} of invertible sheaves and consumes only the
1027	\emph{existence} of the associator, unitor and braiding isomorphisms
1028	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, built by hand as the scheme-theoretic
1029	analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\), not off a coherent monoidal
1030	category's \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\)). The associator
1031	is now obtained directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
1032	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
1033	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), which uses \emph{no} \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\),
1034	\emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), and \emph{no} stalk machinery. Accordingly
1035	the whiskering-stability lemmas and the stalk apparatus of this section are
1036	\emph{vestigial} and must not be re-attempted. The material is kept for the historical record only.
1037	
1038	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
1039	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
1040	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
1041	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
1042	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
1043	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
1044	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
1045	whiskering-stability lemmas below.
1046	
1047	\paragraph{Two-tier strategy.} The group law is assembled by a \emph{two-tier}
1048	strategy, of which this route~(e) is the second tier. The \textbf{primary} tier
1049	builds the commutative group directly on isomorphism classes of \emph{locally
1050	trivial} (invertible) sheaves (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), mirroring
1051	Mathlib's ring-level Picard group \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) /
1052	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). On top of
1053	the locally-trivial-scoped associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
1054	\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} (all
1055	either already proved or locally-trivial-scoped and assembled), it needs only the
1056	restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and the
1057	dual/inverse \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}; it uses \emph{no}
1058	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), \emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), \emph{no}
1059	\(\mathtt{Skeleton}\), and in particular never invokes the stalk--tensor commutation
1060	(d.2). The \textbf{fallback} tier is the full \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to
1061	\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category on all of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)
1062	(this section); it is deferred because its load-bearing whiskering field
1063	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, for an arbitrary whisker object, needs
1064	(d.2) --- the stalk commuting with the relative tensor over a \emph{varying} ring, a
1065	Mathlib-absent ingredient --- and is retained only for the full
1066	monoidal generality on arbitrary modules, which the group law does not consume. The
1067	load-bearing local-injectivity half of \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is thus the sole
1068	remaining open obligation of the \emph{fallback} tier only.
1069	
1070	\emph{Superseded route --- off path, not to be formalized.} The following lemma is
1071	the stalk-linearity packaging supporting the abandoned stalkwise proof of the
1072	whiskering-stability field (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, FALLBACK).
1073	The group law never invokes stalks: the associator is built by direct gluing
1074	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}). This block
1075	is retained for the historical record only and must \emph{not} be formalized.
1076	
1077	\begin{lemma}
1078	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.
1079	
1080	[The $R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x$-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules]
1081	  \label{lem:stalk_linear_map}
1082	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1083	  Let \(X\) be a topological space, \(R\) a presheaf of commutative rings on
1084	  \(X\), and \(x \in X\) a point. For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of presheaves of
1085	  \(R\)-modules, the induced map on stalks
1086	  \[
1087	    g_x \;\colon\; M_x \;\longrightarrow\; N_x
1088	  \]
1089	  is \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\)), and is
1090	  characterised on germs by
1091	  \(g_x(\mathrm{germ}_x\,s) = \mathrm{germ}_x(g_U\,s)\)
1092	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_germ}\)). When the underlying map of
1093	  \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-valued stalks is an isomorphism, \(g_x\) is bijective
1094	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_bijective\_of\_isIso}\)); in that case it bundles to
1095	  an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear equivalence
1096	  \(M_x \xrightarrow{\sim} N_x\) (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso}\)).
1097	
1098	  These four declarations are the \emph{(d.1)-partial implementation} --- the
1099	  linearity half of ingredient (d.1) of
1100	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} --- consumed by the
1101	  \(\mathrm{id} \otimes g_x\) step in the proof of that lemma. They build on the
1102	  stalk module structure of \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk};
1103	  the linearity packaging itself is project-original.
1104	\end{lemma}
1105	
1106	\begin{proof}
1107	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1108	  The stalk \(M_x = \mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) carries its
1109	  \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure from
1110	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk}. The underlying additive map
1111	  \(g_x\) on stalks is the filtered colimit of the section maps \(g_U\); since
1112	  each section map is \(R(U)\)-linear and the scalar action on the stalk is the
1113	  colimit of the section actions, \(g_x\) respects the
1114	  \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-action, which gives the linear map together with its
1115	  germ characterisation. Bijectivity is read off the underlying
1116	  \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk isomorphism (a bijective linear map is a linear
1117	  equivalence), and bundling the inverse yields the linear equivalence.
1118	\end{proof}
1119

1122	\((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) route to the
1123	associator (route~(e)). The group law on iso-classes consumes only the
1124	\emph{existence} of the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), which
1125	is now built directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
1126	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
1127	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) with no whiskering and no stalks. This lemma ---
1128	including both its PRIMARY and FALLBACK proof routes and the stalk ingredients
1129	(d.1)/(d.2) below --- is therefore off the critical path. Retained for the
1130	historical record only.

1132	\begin{lemma}
1133	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.

1136	[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)]
1137	  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1138	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1139	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
1140	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally
1141	  bijective morphisms). Concretely the site carries the
1142	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) property for \(\mathtt{Ab}\), so \(J.W\) is
1143	  exactly the class of \(J\)-locally bijective morphisms; this is recorded as a
1144	  typeclass hypothesis \([J.\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\,\mathtt{Ab}]\) on
1145	  the statement. Let \(F\) be an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of
1146	  \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of
1147	  \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
1148	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R).\mathrm{map}\,g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the
1149	  left-whiskered morphism
1150	  \[
1151	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
1152	  \]
1153	  is \emph{\(J\)-locally injective}. No flatness and no local-triviality
1154	  hypothesis on \(F\) is required.
1155	
1156	  This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the
1157	  instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), the load-bearing
1158	  field of the \emph{full-generality} route~(e) \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) path
1159	  (arbitrary \(F\)). For the relative Picard group law, however, the whisker object
1160	  \(F\) supplied by the sole consumer --- the locally-trivial-scoped associator
1161	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- is always \emph{locally trivial}
1162	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), and in that case local injectivity
1163	  of \(F \triangleleft g\) admits a markedly simpler, stalk-free proof, given as the
1164	  PRIMARY route below. The general stalkwise argument (ingredients (d.1)/(d.2)) is
1165	  retained only as the FALLBACK, required if the full route~(e) monoidal category on
1166	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is later wanted; the locally-trivial
1167	  primary route already suffices for the group law.
1168	\end{lemma}
1169	
1170	\begin{proof}
1171	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:stalk_linear_map,
1172	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1173	
1174	  \emph{PRIMARY route (\(F\) locally trivial --- the consumer's case; sheaf-level,
1175	  no stalks, no (d.2)).} Suppose \(F\) is locally trivial
1176	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), which is exactly the hypothesis its
1177	  sole consumer --- the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- supplies.
1178	  \(J\)-local injectivity of a morphism is \emph{local on a covering family}: it
1179	  suffices to produce a cover of \(X\) on each member of which \(F \triangleleft g\)
1180	  restricts to a locally injective morphism. Take a trivialising open cover
1181	  \(\{V\}\) of \(X\) for \(F\), so that \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) on each \(V\).
1182	  On such a \(V\):
1183	  \begin{itemize}
1184	    \item the substrate--restriction compatibility \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1185	      gives a canonical isomorphism
1186	      \[
1187	        (F \triangleleft g)\big|_V \;\cong\; (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)
1188	      \]
1189	      --- restriction along the open immersion \(V \hookrightarrow X\) commutes with
1190	      \(\otimes\), and the whiskering \(F \triangleleft g = \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g\)
1191	      is \(\otimes\) with an identity, so it restricts to \(\mathrm{id}_{F|_V}
1192	      \otimes (g|_V) = (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)\);
1193	    \item the trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) together with the left
1194	      unitor \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_V \otimes_V (-) \cong
1195	      (-)\)) identifies \((F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V) \cong g|_V\).
1196	  \end{itemize}
1197	  Composing, \((F \triangleleft g)|_V\) is isomorphic to \(g|_V\). Now \(g \in J.W\),
1198	  and membership in \(J.W\) (local bijectivity) is stable under restriction along an
1199	  open immersion, so \(g|_V \in J.W\); in particular \(g|_V\) is \(J\)-locally
1200	  injective. An isomorphism preserves local injectivity, hence \((F \triangleleft
1201	  g)|_V\) is locally injective on \(V\). As the \(\{V\}\) cover \(X\) and local
1202	  injectivity is local on the cover, \(F \triangleleft g\) is \(J\)-locally
1203	  injective. \emph{No stalks and no (d.2) stalk--tensor commutation enter}; the
1204	  single comparison lemma \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (a presheaf-level
1205	  comparison, decomposed into its two open-immersion base-change halves) carries the
1206	  argument, and thereby moves \emph{onto} the critical path.
1207	
1208	  This is \emph{not} the section-level route that tensors a bare section map \(g(V)\)
1209	  --- only injective --- and stalls on a \(\mathrm{Tor}_1\)/flatness obligation. Here
1210	  the argument is sheaf-level and uses that \(g\) is locally \emph{bijective} (a
1211	  \(J.W\) morphism), reduced through the unitor to \(g|_V\) (again a \(J.W\)
1212	  morphism); a mere injection is never tensored, so no flatness is needed.
1213	
1214	  \emph{FALLBACK route (arbitrary \(F\); stalkwise, required only for the
1215	  full-generality route~(e) \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) path).} For an
1216	  \emph{arbitrary} (not necessarily locally trivial) \(F\), local injectivity is
1217	  proved stalkwise, by the argument Mathlib uses for fixed-base presheaves in
1218	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
1219	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
1220	  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
1221	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
1222	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
1223	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
1224	  \[
1225	    (F \triangleleft g)_x
1226	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
1227	      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
1228	  \]
1229	  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
1230	  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
1231	  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
1232	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).
1233	
1234	  The stalkwise computation rests on three ingredients with distinct status at the
1235	  pinned commit:
1236	  \begin{enumerate}
1237	    \item[(d.1-done)] the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear stalk-map packaging
1238	      (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\) and its companions, \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}):
1239	      a morphism of presheaves of modules induces an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear
1240	      map on stalks, characterised on germs, that becomes a linear equivalence once
1241	      the underlying \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk map is an isomorphism. This is built
1242	      project-side, axiom-clean, and furnishes the \(\mathrm{id} \otimes g_x\) step
1243	      above.
1244	    \item[(d.1-bridge)] (remaining) the site-\(J.W\) \(\Longleftrightarrow\)
1245	      stalkwise-iso characterisation on \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\): that a morphism lies
1246	      in \(J.W\) iff its stalk maps are isomorphisms at every point. The concrete
1247	      Mathlib assembly route uses the \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) structure
1248	      together with the topological stalk criteria --- local injectivity
1249	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) injectivity on stalks and local surjectivity
1250	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) surjectivity on stalks --- bridging the site-level
1251	      \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective}\) / \(\mathtt{IsLocallySurjective}\)
1252	      to the \(\mathtt{TopCat.Presheaf}\) stalk versions. This half is still
1253	      Mathlib-absent at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level.
1254	    \item[(d.2)] (remaining) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
1255	      \otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
1256	      module-presheaf tensor, under which \((F \triangleleft g)_x\) is identified
1257	      with \(\mathrm{lTensor}\,F_x\,(g_x) = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) (using
1258	      that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) / \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered
1259	      colimits over a module category). This is genuinely Mathlib-absent over a
1260	      \emph{varying} ring and is the largest remaining piece.
1261	  \end{enumerate}
1262	  Mathlib supplies the analogues for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal concrete
1263	  category, but not for modules over a varying ring; porting (d.1-bridge) and (d.2)
1264	  to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) is the remaining work. Once (d.2) lands, the
1265	  conclusion is immediate and flatness-free: by
1266	  \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_bijective\_of\_isIso}\) the stalk map \(g_x\) is a
1267	  linear equivalence, and the linear-equivalence \(\mathrm{lTensor}\) by \(F_x\)
1268	  carries it to an isomorphism, finishing the proof.
1269	
1270	  This stalkwise fallback is required \emph{only} if the full-generality
1271	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) route~(e) --- the
1272	  monoidal category on \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), for an
1273	  arbitrary whisker object \(F\) --- is later wanted. For the relative Picard group
1274	  law the whisker objects are locally trivial, and the PRIMARY route above suffices,
1275	  trading the Mathlib-absent (d.2) for the single comparison
1276	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
1277	\end{proof}

1287	\begin{lemma}
1288	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.
1289	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
1290	  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}
1291	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1292	  With notation as in \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: for an
1293	  \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) whose
1294	  underlying additive-presheaf map lies in \(J.W\), both whiskerings again lie in
1295	  \(J.W\):
1296	  \[
1297	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; F \triangleleft g \in J.W,
1298	    \qquad
1299	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; g \triangleright F \in J.W .
1300	  \]
1301	  These two statements are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
1302	  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data required by the class
1303	  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) for
1304	  the morphism property \(J.W\); together they furnish the instance
1305	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}. No flatness and no
1306	  local triviality is used --- they are the flatness-free replacements for the
1307	  flat variants of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
1308	\end{lemma}
1309	
1310	\begin{proof}
1311	  \uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1312	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of \(J.W\), it suffices to show
1313	  \(F \triangleleft g\) is both locally injective and locally surjective.
1314	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is free and needs no hypothesis on \(F\): the presheaf
1315	  tensor is right exact, so \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the locally-zero cokernel
1316	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\), which is therefore locally
1317	  zero (Mathlib \(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). \emph{Local
1318	  injectivity} is \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The two together
1319	  give \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
1320	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
1321	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf monoidal structure, which is an isomorphism
1322	  and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\).
1323	\end{proof}

1335	\begin{lemma}
1336	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.
1337	[The \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal substrate (route~(e) target)]
1338	  \label{lem:jw_ismonoidal}
1339	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:whisker_of_W}
1340	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The sheafification localizer \(J.W\) on
1341	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{monoidal}: it carries an
1342	  instance
1343	  \[
1344	    J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal},
1345	  \]
1346	  whose two fields are the whiskering-stability lemmas \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}
1347	  (and which is multiplicative because sheafification inverts identities and
1348	  composites). Consequently, by Mathlib's
1349	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
1350	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) applied to the already-monoidal category
1351	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\), the localization functor
1352	  \(L = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) (which is the localization at
1353	  \(J.W\)), and the unit isomorphism \(L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), the
1354	  category
1355	  \[
1356	    \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
1357	      \;=\; \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1358	  \]
1359	  acquires a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure whose tensor is the substrate
1360	  \(\otimes_X\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) and whose associator,
1361	  left/right unitors, braiding and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
1362	  naturalities) are derived by the API. This is the route~(e) target: the
1363	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
1364	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} are
1365	  then components of this derived monoidal structure rather than hand-assembled
1366	  composites.
1367	
1368	  This block states the route~(e) construction; it depends on the sole open obligation
1369	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. It mirrors Mathlib's
1370	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) construction one categorical level up.
1371	\end{lemma}
1372	
1373	\begin{proof}
1374	  \uses{lem:whisker_of_W}
1375	  Multiplicativity of \(J.W\) (closure under identities and composition) is part
1376	  of its being a localizer (\(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). The two
1377	  whiskering-stability fields of \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) are
1378	  exactly the left- and right-whiskering statements of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
1379	  Hence \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) holds. Sheafification of presheaves of
1380	  modules is a localization at \(J.W\) (the underlying identification of
1381	  sheafification with localization at the locally-bijective class), and the unit
1382	  presheaf sheafifies to \(\mathcal{O}_X\); feeding these to
1383	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) produces the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
1384	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with all coherence derived, as in
1385	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}. No \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure enters
1386	  at any point.
1387	\end{proof}

1389	\begin{lemma}
1390	
1391	\leanok
1392	[Associator for $\otimes_X$ on locally trivial objects]
1393	  \label{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
1394	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso}
1395	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1396	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
1397	  \(M, N, P \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). There is an isomorphism
1398	  \[
1399	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
1400	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1401	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
1402	  \]
1403	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), natural in \(M, N, P\). The group law on
1404	  \(\otimes\)-iso-classes (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only
1405	  the \emph{existence} of this isomorphism, the proposition
1406	  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong M \otimes_X (N
1407	  \otimes_X P)\bigr)\); no pentagon, triangle, or naturality coherence of it is
1408	  ever required.
1409	
1410	  The current Lean pin additionally carries \emph{locally trivial}
1411	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\), \cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial})
1412	  hypotheses on \(M, N, P\). The construction below produces the isomorphism for
1413	  arbitrary \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules; the hypotheses are retained only to match
1414	  the carrier the group law multiplies on, and are not used in the gluing.
1415	\end{lemma}
1416	
1417	\begin{proof}
1418	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1419	  \textbf{Status (route mismatch, deferred).} The \emph{current} realization of this
1420	  associator uses the route-(d) whiskering composite
1421	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W,lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} and the sheafification
1422	  bridge \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}); through
1423	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} --- whose left-whiskering component is
1424	  itself unproved --- it transitively depends on an open proof obligation and is
1425	  therefore \emph{not} axiom-clean. The gluing route described below is the
1426	  \emph{intended} obligation-free realization, but discharging it requires
1427	  \emph{morphism-level descent for \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)}: gluing a family of
1428	  local isomorphisms, agreeing on overlaps, into a single global isomorphism of
1429	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. That is the same descent-infrastructure family
1430	  the \(\otimes\)-inverse needs (\cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}); accordingly, the
1431	  associator re-route \emph{and} the deletion of the vestigial whiskering/stalk
1432	  apparatus are deferred jointly with the dual block, and are not available with the
1433	  current infrastructure. The gluing description below records the target realization.
1434	
1435	  The isomorphism is built by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through the
1436	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, with no
1437	  recourse to any whiskering or stalk apparatus. Fix a
1438	  common open cover \(\{U\}\) of \(X\). On each member \(U\) form the canonical
1439	  composite
1440	  \[
1441	    \bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P\bigr)\big|_U
1442	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1443	    \bigl(M|_U \otimes_U N|_U\bigr) \otimes_U P|_U
1444	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1445	    M|_U \otimes_U \bigl(N|_U \otimes_U P|_U\bigr)
1446	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1447	    \bigl(M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\big|_U ,
1448	  \]
1449	  in which:
1450	  \begin{itemize}
1451	    \item the first arrow is two applications of the restriction-compatibility
1452	      isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} along the open immersion
1453	      \(U \hookrightarrow X\): first \((M \otimes_X N)|_U \cong M|_U \otimes_U
1454	      N|_U\) tensored with \(P|_U\), then \(\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X
1455	      P\bigr)|_U \cong (M \otimes_X N)|_U \otimes_U P|_U\);
1456	    \item the middle arrow is the \emph{canonical presheaf-level associator}
1457	      \(\alpha\) of the (symmetric) monoidal category
1458	      \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_U\)
1459	      (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct.associator}\),
1460	      \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)), read on the restricted modules;
1461	    \item the third arrow is again two applications of
1462	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, in the opposite direction.
1463	  \end{itemize}
1464	  Each of the three arrows is a \emph{canonical} isomorphism: the
1465	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is induced
1466	  by the open immersion with no choices, hence natural in its module arguments, and
1467	  the presheaf associator \(\alpha\) is a fixed natural transformation. Therefore,
1468	  on a double overlap \(U \cap U'\), restricting the \(U\)-composite and the
1469	  \(U'\)-composite to \(U \cap U'\) yields the same morphism: each of the three
1470	  arrows commutes with the further restriction to \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1471	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the presheaf associator, so the two local
1472	  composites agree on the overlap.
1473	
1474	  Because the internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(-,-)\) of sheaves of
1475	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is itself a sheaf on \(X\), a family of morphisms defined
1476	  on the members of an open cover and agreeing on the overlaps glues to a single
1477	  global morphism. Applying this both to the local composites above and to their
1478	  local inverses produces a global morphism
1479	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \to M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\) whose restriction
1480	  to each \(U\) is the local composite, together with a two-sided inverse glued from
1481	  the local inverses; hence the glued morphism is a global isomorphism --- the
1482	  associator. The single non-formal input of this re-route is the
1483	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}; the presheaf
1484	  associator (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)) and the sheaf-of-modules
1485	  internal-hom gluing are already available.
1486	
1487	  Note that, unlike the pointwise-existence statement
1488	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- a \emph{proposition} proved
1489	  cover-pointwise with \emph{no} overlap obligation --- the associator is
1490	  \emph{global data}, a single isomorphism of sheaves on all of \(X\). Assembling it
1491	  from local pieces is therefore genuinely stronger than that pattern: the
1492	  overlap-naturality compatibility of the local composites must be discharged before
1493	  gluing, and is discharged above. The locally-trivial hypotheses of the statement
1494	  are not consumed; the comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is applied to the
1495	  global, untrivialised restrictions \((M \otimes_X N)|_U\), so no free-cover
1496	  specialisation can replace it. The vestigial
1497	  whiskering / \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / stalk apparatus
1498	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W,lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W,lem:jw_ismonoidal}) plays
1499	  no part in this proof and is superseded. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
1500	  consumes only the \emph{existence} of the resulting isomorphism, never its pentagon,
1501	  triangle, or naturality coherence.
1502	\end{proof}

1556	\begin{lemma}
1557	
1558	\leanok
1559	[Inverse of an invertible module]
1560	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
1561	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
1562	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
1563	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1564	  % NOTE: ON the critical path under the PRIMARY locally-trivial group law (it
1565	  % supplies inverses to lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup, in whose uses it sits).
1566	  % Only under the route-(e) fallback is it dispensable: there the group law uses
1567	  % the tensor-invertibility predicate IsInvertible (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible),
1568	  % which carries the inverse existentially, so no separate ``a tensor inverse

1570	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
1571	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2: the dual of an

1576	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1577	  % "\begin{lemma}
1578	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
1579	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1580	  % ...
1581	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
1582	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map

1588	  % \label{definition-pic}

1595	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1596	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
1597	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
1598	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under

1600	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
1601	  bundle. The dual
1602	  \[
1603	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1604	  \]
1605	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
1606	  isomorphism
1607	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).

1610	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
1611	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).

1613	
1614	\begin{proof}
1615	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
1616	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}
1617	  \textbf{Infrastructure-blocked.} This statement is \emph{not} realizable with the
1618	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module infrastructure presently available: its construction
1619	  depends on a \emph{sheaf internal hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules}
1620	  \(\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\), an object that does not yet
1621	  exist at the presheaf, sheaf, or general-categorical level (there is no
1622	  monoidal-closed / internal-hom / dual structure on
1623	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) or \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), and no object-level
1624	  descent assembling a global \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module from local pieces). The dual
1625	  object \(L^{-1}\) therefore cannot even be \emph{named} until that infrastructure
1626	  is built. The construction of the required internal hom, its dual, and its
1627	  evaluation is decomposed into individually-formalizable sub-steps in
1628	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra} (\cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} through
1629	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}); the present lemma is realized once those land,
1630	  and its Lean body is a placeholder until then.
1631	
1632	  We record the intended mathematical route, all three steps of which are correct
1633	  modulo the missing object. \emph{The route is as follows.} Take
1634	  \(L^{-1} := \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\) to be the
1635	  sheaf-level dual of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}, and let
1636	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) be the evaluation of
1637	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}.
1638	
1639	  \emph{Step 1 (\(L^{-1}\) is a line bundle).} Local triviality of \(L\)
1640	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\)) furnishes an open cover \(\{U\}\) of
1641	  \(X\) with trivialisations \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes
1642	  with restriction along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow X\), so on each \(U\)
1643	  \[
1644	    L^{-1}\big|_U \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1645	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1646	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U ,
1647	  \]
1648	  the last identification being the evaluation-at-\(1\) isomorphism for the dual of a
1649	  free rank-one module. Hence \(L^{-1}\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an
1650	  object of \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
1651	
1652	  \emph{Step 2 (the contraction is a local isomorphism).} The evaluation/contraction
1653	  morphism
1654	  \[
1655	    \varepsilon_L \;\colon\; L \otimes_X L^{-1}
1656	      \;=\; L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1657	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_X,
1658	    \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi(s),
1659	  \]
1660	  is a morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Restricting to a trivialising
1661	  open \(U\), the restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1662	  commutes \(\otimes_X\) past \((-)|_U\):
1663	  \[
1664	    (L \otimes_X L^{-1})\big|_U \;\cong\; L|_U \otimes_U L^{-1}|_U
1665	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U ,
1666	  \]
1667	  using the trivialisations of Step~1. Under these identifications \(\varepsilon_L|_U\)
1668	  becomes the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\) of
1669	  the free rank-one module with its dual, which is precisely the left unitor
1670	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\)) and hence an
1671	  isomorphism. Thus \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) is an isomorphism on each member of the cover.
1672	
1673	  \emph{Step 3 (glue to a global isomorphism).} The local trivialisations are induced
1674	  by the open immersions with no choices, so the local contraction isomorphisms
1675	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) agree on the overlaps \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1676	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the unitor under further restriction. As
1677	  ``being an isomorphism'' is local on \(X\) --- a morphism of sheaves of
1678	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose restriction to every member of an open cover is an
1679	  isomorphism is itself an isomorphism --- the globally-defined \(\varepsilon_L\) is an
1680	  isomorphism \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\). Equivalently, the
1681	  local inverses glue (they agree on overlaps for the same reason) to a two-sided
1682	  inverse of \(\varepsilon_L\).
1683	
1684	  Consequently, \emph{once \(L^{-1}\) and \(\varepsilon_L\) are available from
1685	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}}, every line bundle \(L\) has the two-sided tensor
1686	  inverse \(L^{-1}\) under \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes of
1687	  line bundles is an abelian group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1688	  The local-triviality of \(L^{-1}\) (Step~1) is \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} and
1689	  the local-iso\(\Rightarrow\)global-iso passage (Step~3) is the already-closed
1690	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}; the single non-available input is the dual object
1691	  and its evaluation of \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}.
1692	\end{proof}
1693	
1694	\begin{lemma}
1695	
1696	\leanok
1697	[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial carrier
1698	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
1699	  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1700	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
1701	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,
1702	        lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
1703	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. The carrier is the
1704	  \emph{locally-trivial} subtype
1705	  \[
1706	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1707	    \;\;=\;\;
1708	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
1709	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M \,\bigr\}
1710	  \]
1711	  introduced in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. The bifunctor
1712	  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
1713	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
1714	  if \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M'\) then
1715	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\). Hence \(\otimes\)
1716	  closes on \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\), supplying the binary
1717	  operation the relative group law multiplies with.
1718	\end{lemma}
1719	
1720	\begin{proof}
1721	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
1722	  Closure is exactly
1723	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}: the tensor product of two
1724	  locally-trivial rank-one modules is again locally trivial of rank one
1725	  (\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_isLocallyTrivial}\)). The Lean construction
1726	  \(\mathtt{tensorObjOnProduct}\) applies this directly to a pair of objects of
1727	  the \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) subtype \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\),
1728	  returning the tensor with its locally-trivial witness; no
1729	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate and no coherence isomorphism enters this
1730	  closure statement. (The \(\otimes\)-invertibility carrier
1731	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible} is a
1732	  separate predicate, connected to \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) only by an
1733	  off-critical-path equivalence; the units-group packaging is
1734	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, not this lemma.)
1735	\end{proof}
1736	
1737	\begin{lemma}
1738	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
1739	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
1740	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.LineBundle.OnProduct.pullback_tensorObj_iso}
1741	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1742	  % NOTE: pullback preserves the structure sheaf and tensor products (and hence
1743	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
1744	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1745	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1746	  % restriction-compatibility iso.
1747	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
1748	  functor
1749	  \[
1750	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1751	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1752	  \]
1753	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1754	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1755	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1756	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1757	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1758	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1759	  canonical isomorphism
1760	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
1761	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
1762	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
1763	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
1764	\end{lemma}
1765	
1766	\begin{proof}
1767	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1768	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1769	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1770	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1771	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1772	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1773	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1774	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1775	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1776	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1777	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1778	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1779	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
1780	  same cover.
1781	\end{proof}

1783	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1784	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1785	
1786	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1787	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1788	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1789	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1790	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1791	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1792	design template. Under the route~(e) fallback the inverse of an invertible object
1793	is carried by the predicate itself, so neither the open-immersion
1794	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a
1795	separate tensor-inverse lemma is needed there. Under the PRIMARY locally-trivial
1796	route, by contrast, both \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (via the PRIMARY proof
1797	of \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}) and the tensor-inverse lemma
1798	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (supplying inverses to
1799	\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) are on the critical path.

1805	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1809	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4079).
1810	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1811	  % "\begin{definition}
1812	  % \label{definition-invertible}
1813	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
1814	  % {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
1815	  % of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that

1820	  % $$
1821	  % is an equivalence of categories. We say that $\mathcal{L}$ is

1823	  % to $\mathcal{O}_X$.
1824	  % \end{definition}
1825	  % [\dots]
1826	  % \begin{lemma}

1840	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1841	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CS and Lemma~0B8K. An invertible
1842	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is one for which \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
1843	  (-)\) is an equivalence; equivalently there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with
1844	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), and
1845	  then \(\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
1846	  \mathcal{O}_X)\).}
1847	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). We say \(M\)
1848	  is \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertible} when it admits a tensor inverse: there is an
1849	  object \(N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) together with an isomorphism
1850	  \[
1851	    M \otimes_X N \;\cong\;
1852	      \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1853	      \;=\; \mathcal{O}_X ,
1854	  \]
1855	  i.e.\ \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) :\equiv \exists\, N,\
1856	  \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(M \otimes_X N \cong
1857	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)\), with
1858	  \(\otimes_X\) the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The
1859	  predicate is a \emph{proposition} carrying its inverse witness existentially;
1860	  consequently the dual/inverse needed by the group law is definitional and no
1861	  separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is consumed downstream. This is the
1862	  scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\), transported
1863	  one categorical level up to \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with the substrate
1864	  \(\otimes_X\).
1865	\end{definition}

1917	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1918	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1919	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit

1922	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1923	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1924	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1925	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1926	  equivalently, an abelian group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag
1927	  01CR).
1928	
1929	  \textbf{Primary construction (directly on locally-trivial iso-classes, à la
1930	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\)).} The commutative group is built directly on
1931	  isomorphism classes of \emph{locally-trivial} (equivalently \(\otimes\)-invertible)
1932	  sheaves, mirroring Mathlib's ring-level Picard group
1933	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) / \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)
1934	  (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). The group operation is
1935	  \([L]\cdot[M] := [L \otimes_X M]\), well-defined on iso-classes by the
1936	  bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_X\)
1937	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}) and again locally trivial by
1938	  the closure lemma \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}; the identity is
1939	  \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and the inverse of \([L]\) is the class of the dual
1940	  \([L^{-1}]\) supplied by \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (with
1941	  \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)).
1942	
1943	  The four group-axiom fields are defined \emph{by hand}, each discharged by a
1944	  \emph{single} existence-of-isomorphism read as an equality of iso-classes --- passage
1945	  to iso-classes turns an isomorphism of sheaves into an equality of classes:
1946	  \[
1947	    \mathtt{one\_mul} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \lambda_X \rangle,
1948	    \quad
1949	    \mathtt{mul\_one} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \rho_X \rangle,
1950	    \quad
1951	    \mathtt{mul\_assoc} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \alpha_{X,Y,Z} \rangle,
1952	    \quad
1953	    \mathtt{mul\_comm} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \beta_{X,Y} \rangle,
1954	  \]
1955	  where the unit laws are the unitors \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, associativity is
1956	  the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, and commutativity is the braiding
1957	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, each consumed only as the proposition
1958	  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\); the pentagon, triangle and hexagon
1959	  coherence carried by a \(\MonoidalCategory\) typeclass is \emph{never} invoked in
1960	  any of the four axiom proofs.
1961	
1962	  This by-hand assembly does \emph{not} go through
1963	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.monoidOfSkeletalMonoidal}\) or \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\)
1964	  (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Skeleton.lean}): those constructions
1965	  \emph{require} a coherent \([\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}]\) (and, for the commutative
1966	  law, \([\mathtt{BraidedCategory}]\)) on the carrier category --- precisely the
1967	  coherent monoidal category on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) over the \emph{varying}
1968	  structure sheaf that this construction explicitly avoids. Consequently the group law
1969	  requires \emph{no} \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), \emph{no}
1970	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) and \emph{no} \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\) instance on
1971	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The genuine precedent is Mathlib's fixed-ring
1972	  Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
1973	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}):
1974	  there the \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) \emph{is} read off the \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the
1975	  coherent monoidal category \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\)
1976	  that Mathlib supplies over a fixed ring. The present construction \emph{cannot} take
1977	  that \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) route --- no coherent monoidal category on
1978	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) for the varying structure sheaf is available --- so
1979	  its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is built directly from the existence-of-isomorphism
1980	  propositions above, the construction ultimately resting, as
1981	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) does, on iso-classes of invertible modules. The pinned
1982	  carrier \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid}\) realizes this commutative-group
1983	  structure; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at the
1984	  pinned commit.
1985	
1986	  This is the \emph{lower-risk} path: every ingredient is either already proved
1987	  (the unitors \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, the braiding
1988	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}) or locally-trivial-scoped and assembled (the
1989	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}). The only remaining obligations on this
1990	  critical path are the restriction-compatibility comparison
1991	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (which the primary whisker route of
1992	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} also now consumes) and the
1993	  dual/inverse \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}. In particular the group law
1994	  never invokes the stalk--tensor commutation (d.2) over a varying ring.
1995	
1996	  \textbf{Fallback (full route~(e)).} Alternatively, once
1997	  \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} lands, \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X =
1998	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is a full (symmetric)
1999	  \(\MonoidalCategory\) via \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\),
2000	  and the Picard group is the units of its skeleton,
2001	  \(\Pic(X) = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X))\),
2002	  exactly as Mathlib builds \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R =
